visual_features.overall_style: Practical, slightly dated corporate style with strong contrast (black/gold) and bold, all-caps navigation. Visual emphasis is on clarity and utility rather than sleek minimalism. Mix of flat color blocks with simple lines and underlines; very little decoration beyond logo.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven by strong color blocks and typographic contrast. The black top bar draws first attention (white text and bright CTA button), followed by the gold navigation band with the logo on the left. Within the nav, the active item 'CONTACT-US' is emphasized with an underline and slightly bolder/stronger presence. Below, the large centered 'Contact Us' heading in a strong coral-red color stands out against a light grey background. Vertical separators between nav items give each item clear visual weight and separation.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Top bars (black notification + gold navigation) are relatively compact vertically but have good horizontal spacing between items. The content area below the nav uses generous whitespace: the 'Contact Us' heading is isolated in a tall light-grey band, with clear breathing room above and below. Overall, top area is denser; content area is more spacious.
unique_elements: High-contrast black header strip with centered service note and right-aligned phone CTA button.
Gold navigation bar with vertical divider lines between menu items.
Active navigation item styled with a solid underline that is visually offset below the text.
Bold, coral-red page title centered on a light-grey content background.
Large monochrome logo block on gold background (black square with white car illustration and text).

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit centered grid, likely based on a 12-column layout with full-width header/nav and a centered content column inside.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Full-width, stacked horizontal bands: black utility header, gold navigation bar, then a light-grey content area with centered title and subsequent content.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: From top to bottom: (1) Black utility/header strip with site name, notice text, and a prominent call button aligned to the right; (2) Gold navigation band with logo on the left and horizontal nav items spanning the center/right; (3) Main content area starting with a large centered 'Contact Us' page title on a light-grey background; further sections presumably follow below in vertical sequence.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Header and nav are horizontally full-width with text either centered (top notice) or left/right-aligned (logo left, CTA right). Navigation items are center-aligned within the gold band with consistent spacing and vertical separators. The page title is centered both horizontally and vertically within its band, reinforcing a strong central axis.
responsive_hints: Top-level structure uses clear horizontal bands that adapt easily to different widths.
Navigation is linear and could collapse into a menu on smaller screens; current screenshot shows desktop with ample horizontal space.
CTA button at top-right is isolated enough to stack below or above text on mobile without losing clarity.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow with full-width horizontal sections. The first two sections (black header and gold nav) are dense, utility-focused bars. Below, the main content area starts with a centered page title on a light-grey canvas, leaving a large vertical gap before the next content block, suggesting subsequent sections (forms, cards, contact details) are stacked in a single-column layout under this heading.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Roughly the top 20–25% of the initial viewport is occupied by the black utility bar and gold nav together. The remaining majority is the light-grey content area where the 'Contact Us' title sits with generous vertical spacing. The logo block occupies about the left quarter of the gold nav, while the navigation menu spans the center/right; the 'CALL US!' button is visually separated within the black header on the right with sufficient padding so it stands out.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately dense at the very top (text and CTA compressed within a narrow black strip), followed by a medium-density navigation bar, then a spacious content area with ample whitespace around the page title. Overall impression is functional but not cluttered; the primary content area feels open and easy to scan.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Initial eye movement follows a horizontal F/Z-pattern: start at the top-left brand name in the black bar, move across to the booking notice and then to the bright 'CALL US!' button on the top-right. Next, the eye drops to the gold nav bar, scanning from the logo on the left across the navigation links to the active 'CONTACT-US' item. From there, the strong coral-red 'Contact Us' heading draws the eye down into the main content area.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary CTA ('CALL US!') is a rectangular button with slightly rounded corners (~4–6px radius). It uses a black outer container with a strong white border and a bright yellow/orange inner fill for the button face, plus a phone icon on the left and all-caps label on the right. Text appears white or very light for contrast against the orange fill. The style is flat with strong contrast; no heavy gradients, possibly a subtle inner shadow implied by the two-tone (black outer, yellow inner) structure.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely implemented as a <button> or an <a> styled as a button, wrapped in a container div to achieve the double-layer effect (outer black container with border, inner colored area). Internally it probably uses an inline SVG or icon font for the phone symbol followed by span text. The button sits within the header as a right-aligned element.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Not explicitly visible in the provided viewport; however, the nav and logo area suggest a preference for strong rectangular blocks with clear borders and no heavy shadows. Expected card style would be sharp or minimally rounded rectangles, light background (white or light grey), slender 1px border in a neutral color, and moderate internal padding (~16–24px). Emphasis likely on flat design with clear separation via color blocks.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typical card structure would be a <div> wrapper with optional heading, content area, and perhaps a button or link row at the bottom. Layout may follow a simple vertical stack: title, text, optional icons or CTA. Could be implemented as semantic <section> or <article> elements in content-heavy areas such as 'For Business' or booking info.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The contact page heading suggests a form below, though not visible here. Based on the rest of the UI, form fields are likely full-width inputs with rectangular shapes, light backgrounds, and thin dark borders. Labels are expected to be placed above each input (stacked layout) for clarity. Buttons would match the primary CTA styling but possibly simplified (solid color with white text). Validation likely uses red text or border color changes.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms are probably organized as stacked blocks with labels as <label> elements above <input> or <textarea>. Grouping may be done via <div> wrappers or <fieldset> blocks for business vs personal sections. Submit buttons likely appear at the bottom of each form section in a full-width or centered layout.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Horizontal, top-level navigation bar on gold background. Menu items are uppercase sans-serif text in white, evenly spaced with vertical black separator lines between them. The active item 'CONTACT-US' is underlined with a solid black line positioned slightly below the baseline, visually thicker than an ordinary text underline. Typography is bold enough to stand against the gold background. The navigation overall feels rigid and structured.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely implemented as a <nav> containing an unordered list (<ul>) of list items (<li>) with links (<a>). Separators may be either border-right on each <li> or separate elements between items. The active page likely uses an 'active' class to add the bottom border/underline. The entire nav is wrapped inside a header div with the logo block on the left and the list floated or flexed to the right/center.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Not directly visible; however, the nav and header suggest list treatments with clear separation, vertical dividers, and uppercase or bold labels. For contact details or information lists, items are likely presented in a medium-density style with enough spacing between lines and possibly bullet points or icons aligned to the left. Dividers may be thin 1px lines similar to vertical nav separators.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Standard HTML <ul> or <ol> lists with <li> items, potentially styled using flexbox for media-style layouts (icon left, text right). For more tabular information (e.g., fares or destinations), they may use table-like rows or flex rows with consistent alignment of labels and values.

heading.visual_style: Bold, all-caps sans-serif with fairly uniform stroke width, giving a neutral, slightly condensed corporate look. High legibility, no serifs, and relatively tight tracking.

body.visual_style: Clean, neutral sans-serif similar to system defaults, suitable for longer text blocks and small labels.

spacing_system.component_padding: Navigation items and buttons appear to use around 8–16px horizontal padding and 8–12px vertical padding. Logo block has larger inner padding (~16–24px) to give it prominence against the gold bar.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., nav bar and 'Contact Us' header, or header and subsequent form/content) appears to be in the 32–48px range, allowing distinct separation and clear scanning.

interaction_hints: Navigation items likely have hover states with either a color change (e.g., text from white to a darker shade or underline appearing) and possibly boldening to reinforce interactivity.
The primary CTA button may have a hover effect such as slightly darkening the yellow/orange fill, increasing box-shadow, or subtle scale-up to emphasize clickability.
Active navigation item already uses an underline; on click or focus, it may maintain this underline and potentially change text color.

Section transitions are probably simple and instantaneous; subtle fade or slide-in animations could be added when scrolling to improve perceived polish without clashing with the straightforward visual style.

Form elements (on the contact page) likely show focus states with darker borders or glow effects; error validation feedback should use the red semantic color and perhaps small helper text beneath fields.
